name: 伊戈尔·斯特拉文斯基
brief: 斯特拉文斯基出生于圣彼得堡附近的奥拉宁鲍姆，父亲是马林斯基剧院的著名男低音。他师从里姆斯基-科萨科夫，被芭蕾舞剧企业家迪亚基列夫发现后一举成名。他一生经历了三次风格巨变——从俄罗斯原始主义到新古典主义再到序列主义——每一次转变都震惊了音乐界。他先后定居瑞士、法国和美国，1971年在纽约去世，根据遗愿被安葬在威尼斯。
briefEn: Stravinsky was born in Oranienbaum, near St. Petersburg; his father was a celebrated bass at the Mariinsky Theatre. He studied with Rimsky-Korsakov and rocketed to fame after being discovered by the ballet impresario Diaghilev. Over his career he underwent three seismic stylistic shifts — from Russian primitivism to Neoclassicism to serialism — each one sending shockwaves through the musical world. He lived successively in Switzerland, France, and the United States, dying in New York in 1971. In accordance with his wishes, he was buried in Venice.
contribution: 斯特拉文斯基的《春之祭》（1913）是音乐史上最具颠覆性的作品——它的首演在巴黎引发了真正的骚乱。他以前所未有的方式解放了节奏——不规则的重音、频繁变换的拍号、多层节奏的叠加创造了一种原始而暴烈的音乐力量。他的新古典主义时期则证明了现代作曲家可以创造性地回望传统，而不是一味追求「进步」。
contributionEn: Stravinsky's The Rite of Spring (1913) is the most revolutionary work in music history — its Paris premiere literally provoked a riot. He liberated rhythm in ways never before imagined: irregular accents, constantly shifting time signatures, and polyrhythmic layering generated a primordial, explosive musical force. His Neoclassical period proved that a modern composer could look creatively backward to tradition rather than pursuing "progress" at all costs.
style: 斯特拉文斯基的风格以「客观性」著称——他拒绝浪漫主义的情感泛滥，追求音乐作为纯粹的「声音的游戏」。他的早期作品以原始节奏的蛮力和管弦乐色彩的辉煌著称；新古典主义时期的作品以冷静的幽默、精练的形式和反讽的距离感为特征；晚年的序列作品则展现出一种稀薄而透明的声音美学。贯穿始终的是他对节奏的天才掌控和对管弦乐音色的无穷想象力。
styleEn: Stravinsky's style is defined by its objectivity — he rejected Romantic emotional excess, treating music as a pure "play of sounds." His early works are marked by the brute force of primitive rhythm and brilliant orchestral color; his Neoclassical works by cool wit, lean formal design, and ironic detachment; his late serial pieces by a spare, transparent sonic aesthetic. Throughout every phase, two constants remain: his genius for rhythm and his inexhaustible imagination for orchestral timbre.

famousScores.name: 《春之祭》 Le Sacre du printemps
famousScores.desc: 1913年首演引发骚乱，节奏革命的圣经
famousScores.descEn: Its 1913 premiere sparked a riot — the sacred text of the rhythmic revolution
famousScores.detail:
1913年5月29日晚，巴黎香榭丽舍剧院，指挥皮埃尔·蒙特（Pierre Monteux）举起指挥棒，尼金斯基的芭蕾舞剧《春之祭》开演。大幕刚落，观众席就响起了压低的嘲笑声和口哨声——来自巴松管的高音区开场旋律实在太过陌生。随着音乐进行，骚动升级为真正的骚乱：保守的传统派与支持先锋艺术的前卫派之间爆发了肢体冲突，人们相互推搡、谩骂，甚至动手打架。有记载称，整个剧场的噪音大到使台上的乐手几乎听不到彼此的演奏，蒙特已无法通过眼神与乐手交流。后台的斯特拉文斯基，据他后来回忆，不得不抓住尼金斯基的衣服，大声在他耳边数拍子，帮助他在台上指导舞者。

演出结束时，部分观众离场，部分观众留下鼓掌。第二天的评论界几乎全体哗然——有人称之为「不知所云的噪音」，有人称之为「音乐的犯罪」，但也有人当即意识到历史性的时刻已经到来。作曲家德彪西（当时与斯特拉文斯基交好）据说在演出结束后神色复杂地离开——他意识到这部作品将彻底改变音乐的走向，而他自己的印象主义或许已成过去。

《春之祭》的音乐革命集中在一点：节奏。从「春天的预兆」开始，频繁变换的拍号（2/4、3/4、5/4、7/8……有时每小节都在变）和不规则的重音，使音乐获得了一种原始的、难以预测的力量。这部作品改变了整个西方音乐对节奏的理解，是任何关于20世纪音乐的讨论都无法回避的核心参照点。
famousScores.detailEn:
On the evening of May 29, 1913, at the Théâtre des Champs-Élysées in Paris, conductor Pierre Monteux raised his baton and the curtain rose on Nijinsky's ballet of The Rite of Spring. Before the first page was finished, suppressed laughter and hissing rose from the audience — the opening bassoon melody in its highest register sounded impossibly strange. As the music proceeded, disturbance escalated into genuine riot: physical confrontations broke out between conservative traditionalists and supporters of the avant-garde, with pushing, shouting, and fighting in the stalls. Accounts record that the uproar grew so loud that the musicians on stage could barely hear each other; Monteux could no longer make eye contact with his players. Backstage, Stravinsky — by his own later account — had to grab Nijinsky by his clothing and shout counts directly into his ear to help him cue the dancers on stage.

When the performance ended, part of the audience left and part applauded. The next day's critics were nearly unanimous in uproar — some called it "noise without meaning," some "a musical crime" — but a few recognized immediately that a historic moment had arrived. Debussy (then still on friendly terms with Stravinsky) reportedly left afterward looking deeply conflicted, recognizing that this work would alter the direction of music entirely and that his own Impressionism might already belong to the past.

The Rite of Spring's musical revolution converges on a single point: rhythm. From the "Augurs of Spring" onward, constantly shifting time signatures (2/4, 3/4, 5/4, 7/8... sometimes changing every bar) and irregular accents give the music a primordial, unpredictable force. This work transformed the entire Western understanding of rhythm and is the unavoidable central reference point of any discussion of twentieth-century music.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Igor Stravinsky, Pierre Boulez, The Cleveland Orchestra
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Rite Of Spring - Part 1: Adoration Of The Earth

famousScores.name: 《火鸟》
famousScores.desc: 第一部芭蕾舞剧，管弦乐配器的杰出范例
famousScores.descEn: His first ballet and a dazzling showcase of orchestral color
famousScores.detail:
《火鸟》（1910）是斯特拉文斯基为迪亚基列夫的俄罗斯芭蕾舞团创作的第一部芭蕾，也是他一举成名的作品。作品以俄罗斯民间故事为素材，讲述了伊万王子捕获神鸟火鸟、借助它的羽毛打败魔王卡谢依的故事。斯特拉文斯基从他的导师里姆斯基-科萨科夫处继承了对民间音乐和管弦乐色彩的极度敏感，在这部作品中将其发展到了令人叹为观止的高度。

《火鸟》的配器手法至今被视为示范级别的典范——火鸟的主题用弦乐泛音和钢片琴描绘，卡谢依魔宫的音乐则以刺耳的铜管和打击乐刻画，凡人世界的民间旋律用木管和弦乐表现——不同的声音世界通过配器手段被截然区分，整部芭蕾如同一部管弦乐教科书。

1910年6月25日在巴黎首演，立即引发轰动，斯特拉文斯基一夜之间成为国际音乐界最耀眼的新星。这部作品后来被改编成三首不同规模的管弦乐组曲，至今是音乐厅最常演出的作品之一。它标志着一位作曲家天才的第一次震撼亮相，也宣告了一种充满活力的俄罗斯现代音乐语言的到来。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Firebird (1910) was Stravinsky's first ballet for Diaghilev's Ballets Russes and the work that made him famous overnight. Based on Russian folk tales, it tells of Prince Ivan who captures the magical Firebird and uses its feather to defeat the sorcerer Kashchei. Stravinsky inherited from his teacher Rimsky-Korsakov an extraordinary sensitivity to folk music and orchestral color, developing both to breathtaking heights in this work.

The Firebird's orchestration is still regarded as a model of its kind: the Firebird's theme is painted in string harmonics and celesta; Kashchei's palace music is etched in harsh brass and percussion; the human world's folk melodies are voiced in woodwinds and strings — different sonic worlds are sharply differentiated through instrumental means, making the complete ballet read almost like an orchestration textbook.

The premiere in Paris on June 25, 1910 caused an immediate sensation, making Stravinsky the most dazzling new star in the international musical firmament overnight. Three concert suites of different scales have since been arranged from the score, and the work remains one of the most frequently performed in the orchestral concert hall. It announced the first shattering entrance of a compositional genius and proclaimed the arrival of a vital new Russian modern musical language.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Igor Stravinsky, Eugene Ormandy, Philadelphia Orchestra
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Mussorgsky: Pictures at an Exhibition - Stravinsky: L'Oiseau de feu Suite

famousScores.name: 《彼得鲁什卡》
famousScores.desc: 木偶戏芭蕾，双调性的经典运用
famousScores.descEn: A puppet-show ballet and a classic use of bitonality
famousScores.detail:
《彼得鲁什卡》（1911）是斯特拉文斯基三大芭蕾中音乐语言最为丰富、最富于人性的一部。故事发生在圣彼得堡的嘉年华集市上：三个木偶——彼得鲁什卡（忧郁的小丑）、芭蕾舞女、摩尔人——在魔术师的控制下上演了一场三角恋悲剧，结局是彼得鲁什卡被摩尔人刺死。斯特拉文斯基在音乐中以双调性（C大调与升F大调同时响起的「彼得鲁什卡和弦」）描绘木偶世界与人类世界的对立。

「彼得鲁什卡和弦」——C大调与升F大调三和弦的叠置——是音乐史上最著名的双调性例子之一，也是斯特拉文斯基对传统和声规则最大胆的冲击之一。这个和弦如今在和声学教材中被广泛引用，并以这部作品命名。作品中大量俄罗斯民间旋律的运用，与现代和声语言形成了独特的张力。

1911年6月13日在巴黎首演，由尼金斯基主演彼得鲁什卡，再次大获成功。更值得一提的是，《彼得鲁什卡》后来被拉威尔（与斯特拉文斯基交好且相互尊重）改编为著名的钢琴独奏版《三首彼得鲁什卡片段》，成为钢琴文献中最难、最著名的作品之一——斯特拉文斯基对此表示高度赞赏。
famousScores.detailEn:
Petrushka (1911) is the most humanly rich of Stravinsky's three great ballets. Set at a St. Petersburg carnival, the story follows three puppets — Petrushka (the melancholy clown), the Ballerina, and the Moor — in a tragic love triangle under a magician's control, ending with Petrushka stabbed to death by the Moor. Stravinsky uses bitonality (the simultaneous sounding of C major and F-sharp major, the "Petrushka chord") to portray the opposition between the puppet world and the human world.

The "Petrushka chord" — the superimposition of C major and F-sharp major triads — is one of the most celebrated examples of bitonality in music history, and one of Stravinsky's boldest challenges to conventional harmonic rules. The chord is now widely cited in harmony textbooks and bears the work's name. The extensive use of Russian folk melodies throughout creates a distinctive tension with the modern harmonic language.

The premiere in Paris on June 13, 1911 with Nijinsky in the title role was another tremendous success. Notably, Ravel (a mutual admirer) later transcribed Petrushka into the famous piano solo Three Movements from Petrushka — one of the most technically difficult and celebrated pieces in the piano literature — and Stravinsky expressed his great admiration for this arrangement.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Igor Stravinsky, Valery Gergiev, Mariinsky Orchestra
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Petrushka: First Scene: I. The Shrovetide Fair (Introduction) (1911 original version)

famousScores.name: 《诗篇交响曲》
famousScores.desc: 新古典主义时期的宗教杰作，庄严而超脱
famousScores.descEn: A Neoclassical sacred masterpiece — solemn and transcendent
famousScores.detail:
《诗篇交响曲》（1930）是斯特拉文斯基受波士顿交响乐团委托、为其成立50周年而作的宗教作品，也是他新古典主义时期最重要的杰作之一。作品以拉丁文《圣咏》（诗篇38、39和150篇）为文本，由合唱团和管弦乐队演出——但管弦乐队特意去掉了小提琴和中提琴，以创造一种更为古朴、更接近古代音乐质感的声音。

斯特拉文斯基在创作这部作品时，明确地以巴赫和亨德尔为精神参照——但他不是在模仿，而是以现代的眼光重新创造古代精神。第二乐章以一个双重赋格展开，展现了他对巴洛克对位技法的深刻掌握；第三乐章则以庄严的颂歌（Allelujah）开篇，最终归于一种近乎神圣的静默——那段缓慢的、以E大调结束的「阿门」，是斯特拉文斯基整个创作中最令人动容的时刻之一。

首演于1930年12月19日在布鲁塞尔，指挥恩斯特·安塞美（Ernest Ansermet），随后在波士顿由科谢维茨基指挥首演美国版。《诗篇交响曲》是斯特拉文斯基宗教信仰（他于1920年代重归东正教）最直接的音乐表达，也是新古典主义音乐的最高成就之一，证明了现代作曲家同样可以以古老语言写出直达心灵的音乐。
famousScores.detailEn:
Symphony of Psalms (1930) was commissioned by the Boston Symphony Orchestra for its fiftieth anniversary and is one of the most important masterpieces of Stravinsky's Neoclassical period. Setting the Latin text of Psalms 38, 39, and 150 for chorus and orchestra — with violins and violas specifically omitted to create a more archaic, ancient-sounding texture — it is a work of strikingly spare severity.

In composing this work Stravinsky took Bach and Handel as his spiritual reference points — yet he was not imitating but recreating the ancient spirit through a modern sensibility. The second movement unfolds as a double fugue, demonstrating his deep command of Baroque contrapuntal technique; the third movement opens with a solemn Alleluia before arriving at a near-divine stillness — that slow, E major "Amen" is one of the most deeply moving moments in Stravinsky's entire output.

The premiere took place in Brussels on December 19, 1930 under Ernest Ansermet, followed by the American premiere in Boston under Koussevitzky. The Symphony of Psalms is the most direct musical expression of Stravinsky's religious faith (he had returned to Russian Orthodoxy in the 1920s), and one of the supreme achievements of Neoclassical music, demonstrating that a modern composer can speak directly to the heart in the ancient language of sacred music.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Igor Stravinsky, Leonard Bernstein, English Bach Festival Choir, London Symphony Orchestra
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Shapero: Symphony for Classical Orchestra - Stravinsky: Symphony of Psalms
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Symphony of Psalms: I. Exaudi orationem meam, Domine

famousScores.name: 《士兵的故事》
famousScores.desc: 七件乐器与朗诵者，室内乐剧的先驱
famousScores.descEn: Seven instruments and a narrator — a pioneering work of chamber music theater
famousScores.detail:
《士兵的故事》（1918）是斯特拉文斯基在第一次世界大战期间流亡瑞士时创作的，创作背景极为困难——他与迪亚基列夫分道扬镳，经济拮据，在战争导致的出版和演出危机中寻求一种「最小化」的生存方式。他与作家雷慕斯·冈扎（C.F. Ramuz）合作，共同创作了这部只需七件乐器（小提琴、低音提琴、单簧管、巴松管、小号、长号和打击乐）、三位朗诵者和舞蹈演员的「讲演、演奏和舞蹈」作品。

故事改编自俄罗斯民间故事，讲述一个士兵把小提琴卖给魔鬼、换取财富与权力，最终却失去了音乐和灵魂的寓言。斯特拉文斯基在配乐中大量使用爵士元素（当时他通过乐谱接触到拉格泰姆和探戈，尚未亲耳听过爵士乐）、探戈、圆舞曲和进行曲，以七件乐器创造出惊人的音色多样性。

这部作品是斯特拉文斯基新古典主义风格的早期预兆，也是20世纪「音乐剧场」概念的重要先驱。它证明了在最有限的资源下，音乐依然可以讲述最深刻的故事。七件乐器的编制成为后来无数室内乐剧的参考范本，作品本身至今仍在世界各地频繁演出，其中的「士兵的进行曲」和「皇家进行曲」已成为独立的音乐会曲目。
famousScores.detailEn:
L'Histoire du soldat (1918) was composed during Stravinsky's wartime exile in Switzerland under conditions of acute difficulty — he had parted ways with Diaghilev, was financially constrained, and was seeking a "minimal" path through a crisis of publishing and performance caused by the war. In collaboration with writer C.F. Ramuz, he created this work for narrator, dancers, and seven instruments only (violin, double bass, clarinet, bassoon, trumpet, trombone, and percussion).

The story, adapted from Russian folk tales, tells of a soldier who sells his violin to the Devil in exchange for wealth and power, only to lose both his music and his soul. Stravinsky's score draws heavily on jazz elements (at this time he had encountered ragtime and tango only through sheet music, never having actually heard jazz), alongside tangos, waltzes, and marches, achieving astonishing timbral variety from just seven instruments.

This work is an early harbinger of Stravinsky's Neoclassical style and an important pioneer of the twentieth-century "music theater" concept. It demonstrates that with the most limited resources, music can still tell the most profound stories. Its seven-instrument ensemble became a reference model for countless subsequent chamber theater works, and the piece itself is still frequently performed worldwide, with the "Soldier's March" and "Royal March" now established as independent concert items.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Igor Stravinsky, Christopher Lee, Lionel Friend, Scottish Chamber Orchestra
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: The Soldier's Tale, Part One: Introduction, The Soldier's March

famousScores.name: 《普钦尼拉》（新古典主义芭蕾）
famousScores.desc: 以佩戈莱西等人的巴洛克音乐为素材，新古典主义风格的宣言之作
famousScores.descEn: Reworking Baroque material by Pergolesi and others — the manifesto of Neoclassicism
famousScores.detail:
《普钦尼拉》（1919—1920）是斯特拉文斯基新古典主义风格的「宣言之作」，也是他与毕加索（布景与服装设计）的重要合作。迪亚基列夫说服斯特拉文斯基将18世纪意大利作曲家佩戈莱西（Giovanni Battista Pergolesi）及其同时代人的巴洛克作品改编为芭蕾，斯特拉文斯基起初有些勉强，最终却被这些原材料深深吸引，创造出一种「透过现代棱镜重新折射的古代音乐」。

他的方法不是忠实复原，而是创造性的误读——原始旋律被保留，但和声被巧妙地「错位」，节奏被轻微扭曲，配器被替换为室内乐规模的小型管弦乐队。这些微小的扭曲使原本熟悉的巴洛克旋律既亲切又陌生，如同透过波纹玻璃看到的过去。斯特拉文斯基后来回忆说，在接触这些佩戈莱西的原稿时，他「感到自己触摸到了过去的手」。

1920年5月15日在巴黎歌剧院首演，毕加索的立体主义舞台布景与斯特拉文斯基的音乐相得益彰，再次引发轰动。《普钦尼拉》宣告了斯特拉文斯基第二个重要风格阶段的到来，也开创了整个「新古典主义」音乐运动的方向——它告诉所有听到它的人：向过去学习，并不意味着放弃现代性。
famousScores.detailEn:
Pulcinella (1919–20) is the "manifesto" of Stravinsky's Neoclassical style and an important collaboration with Picasso (set and costume design). Diaghilev persuaded Stravinsky to arrange Baroque music by the eighteenth-century Italian composer Pergolesi and contemporaries for ballet. Stravinsky was initially reluctant but became deeply fascinated by the source material, creating what might be described as "ancient music refracted through a modern prism."

His approach was not faithful restoration but creative misreading — original melodies were retained, but harmonies subtly "dislocated," rhythms gently twisted, and orchestration reduced to a small chamber orchestra. These small distortions make the familiar Baroque melodies simultaneously intimate and strange, like the past seen through rippled glass. Stravinsky later recalled that when he encountered the Pergolesi manuscripts he "felt as though he were shaking hands with the past."

The premiere at the Paris Opéra on May 15, 1920, with Picasso's Cubist stage design complementing Stravinsky's music, caused another sensation. Pulcinella announced the arrival of Stravinsky's second important stylistic phase and pointed the direction for the entire Neoclassicist movement in music — it told everyone who heard it that looking to the past need not mean abandoning modernity.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Igor Stravinsky, Sir Neville Marriner, Academy of St. Martin in the Fields
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Stravinsky Work of Art - Compilation by Igor Stravinsky

famousScores.name: 《俄狄浦斯王》
famousScores.desc: 拉丁语演唱的歌剧-清唱剧，以古典冷静面对最深重的悲剧
famousScores.descEn: An opera-oratorio in Latin — Classical serenity confronting the most crushing human tragedy
famousScores.detail:
《俄狄浦斯王》（1926—1927）是斯特拉文斯基新古典主义时期最重要的声乐作品，以科克托改编、达尼埃尔·伽拉尼翻译为拉丁语的索福克勒斯悲剧为文本，创作为一部「歌剧-清唱剧」。斯特拉文斯基故意选择了一种「纪念碑」式的、非人格化的演出方式：演唱者保持相对静止的姿势（不像普通歌剧那样有大量动作），叙述者以现代语言（观众的语言）解说，而演唱者则以拉丁语演唱，制造了一种刻意的古典距离感。

这种距离感并非冷漠，而是一种古希腊悲剧意义上的庄严——当俄狄浦斯（Oedipus Rex）一步步走向命运的揭露时，斯特拉文斯基的音乐以一种令人窒息的必然性推进，每一句合唱都如同古希腊神庙上的石柱，不可动摇。「俄狄浦斯！俄狄浦斯！」这句反复出现的合唱呼喊，成为了整个西方悲剧传统在20世纪音乐中的最强烈回响之一。

1927年5月30日在巴黎以音乐会形式首演（舞台版于1928年在维也纳），指挥斯特拉文斯基本人，首演时迪亚基列夫在场，后者对这部「太缺少舞台动作」的作品相当不满。然而今天，《俄狄浦斯王》被视为斯特拉文斯基新古典主义作品中最有深度、最能经受时间考验的杰作之一，以其对悲剧美学的完美处理，在20世纪声乐音乐史上占据了独特地位。
famousScores.detailEn:
Oedipus Rex (1926–27) is the most important vocal work of Stravinsky's Neoclassical period, setting a text adapted by Cocteau from Sophocles's tragedy and translated into Latin by Jean Daniélou, composed as an "opera-oratorio." Stravinsky deliberately chose a "monumental," depersonalized mode of presentation: singers hold relatively still postures (unlike conventional opera), a narrator explains events in the audience's own modern language, while the singers perform in Latin, creating an intentional classical distance.

This distance is not coldness but solemnity in the Greek tragic sense — as Oedipus moves step by step toward the revelation of his fate, Stravinsky's music advances with breathtaking inevitability, each choral passage rising like a stone column of an ancient Greek temple, immovable. The recurring choral cry "Oedipus! Oedipus!" becomes one of the most powerful echoes of the entire Western tragic tradition in twentieth-century music.

The premiere in Paris on May 30, 1927 (staged version in Vienna in 1928) was conducted by Stravinsky himself; Diaghilev, present at the premiere, was considerably dissatisfied with the work's "lack of stage action." Today, however, Oedipus Rex is regarded as one of the most profoundly conceived and time-resistant of all Stravinsky's Neoclassical works, holding a unique place in the history of twentieth-century vocal music for the perfection of its handling of tragic aesthetics.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Igor Stravinsky, Edward Fox, Martyn Hill, Simon Joly Male Chorus, Philharmonia Orchestra, Robert Craft
famousScores.canonicalAlbum: Discover Opera - Compilation by Various Artists

famousScores.name: 《三个乐章交响曲》
famousScores.desc: 二战的音乐回响，斯特拉文斯基对战争的独特见证
famousScores.descEn: The musical echo of World War II — Stravinsky's unique witness to the conflict
famousScores.detail:
《三个乐章交响曲》（1942—1945）是斯特拉文斯基二战时期在美国创作的重要作品，也是他整个新古典主义时期最具棱角、最充满节奏活力的交响曲。他后来承认，这部交响曲中有对战争的直接反应——第一乐章的某些段落是看到中国军队与日本军队交战的纪录片影像后写成的，第三乐章则有对德国军队入侵场景（摘自电影画面）的联想。

但斯特拉文斯基并非在创作「战争音乐」——他对描绘性音乐始终保持警惕。这些外部联想只是作曲的起点；最终的交响曲是纯粹的音乐建筑，以一种比以往更加刚健、更加节奏化的语言构筑。第一乐章尤其以惊人的节奏力量和紧凑的对位织体著称，甚至在某些地方让人联想起《春之祭》的早期节奏精神。

1946年1月24日在纽约由纽约爱乐乐团首演，作曲家本人指挥，反应热烈。这部作品标志着斯特拉文斯基美国时期最重要的管弦乐成就之一，它以冷静的形式智慧处理了历史性的创伤，在调性与现代和声之间找到了一种与众不同的个人平衡点。
famousScores.detailEn:
Symphony in Three Movements (1942–45) is an important work Stravinsky composed in the United States during the Second World War, and the most angular, rhythmically vital symphony of his entire Neoclassical period. He later acknowledged that the symphony contains direct responses to the war — certain passages of the first movement were written after watching documentary footage of Chinese and Japanese forces in combat, while the third movement was associated with images of German military advance seen in film.

Yet Stravinsky was never composing "war music" — he remained wary of descriptive composition throughout his career. These external associations were merely starting points; the finished symphony is pure musical architecture, built in a language more vigorous and rhythmically charged than anything he had previously written. The first movement in particular is remarkable for its stunning rhythmic power and dense contrapuntal texture, at moments recalling the early rhythmic spirit of The Rite of Spring.

The premiere on January 24, 1946 in New York by the New York Philharmonic under the composer himself was warmly received. The work marks one of the most important orchestral achievements of Stravinsky's American period, handling historical trauma through cool formal intelligence and finding a uniquely personal balance between tonality and modern harmony.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Igor Stravinsky, Sir Simon Rattle, Berliner Philharmoniker
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Stravinsky: Symphony in Three Movements: I. Overture. Allegro

famousScores.name: 《浪子历程》（歌剧）
famousScores.desc: 霍加斯版画启发的新古典主义歌剧，与奥登合作的英语歌剧杰作
famousScores.descEn: Inspired by Hogarth's prints, in collaboration with Auden — the Neoclassical opera par excellence
famousScores.detail:
《浪子历程》（1948—1951）是斯特拉文斯基最后一部新古典主义大型作品，也是他一生中唯一的全本英语歌剧。剧本由英国大诗人W.H.奥登（W.H. Auden）和切斯特·卡尔曼（Chester Kallman）合作撰写，灵感来源于英国画家威廉·霍加斯（William Hogarth）的一组18世纪版画系列《浪子历程》，讲述一个年轻人受到恶魔塔德·尼克（Nick Shadow）的诱惑而堕落、最终疯癫的道德寓言。

斯特拉文斯基以莫扎特式的简洁配器和意大利巴洛克歌剧的咏叹调结构为框架，构建了这部充满机巧和反讽的歌剧。正如他对佩戈莱西的处理一样，他对莫扎特式语汇的运用也是一种「创造性误读」——和声常常以微妙的方式偏离调性期待，节奏有时呈现出非古典的顿挫，而音乐整体上保持着一种冷静而洗练的优雅。

1951年9月11日在威尼斯凤凰歌剧院由作曲家本人指挥世界首演，正是在这里，斯特拉文斯基仅三周后邂逅了指挥家罗伯特·克拉夫特（Robert Craft），后者成为他晚年最重要的合作者。《浪子历程》是新古典主义歌剧的代表作，也是20世纪英语歌剧中最重要的作品之一，以其精炼的音乐机巧和深刻的道德寓言，在歌剧史上确立了不可动摇的地位。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Rake's Progress (1948–51) is Stravinsky's last major Neoclassical work and the only full-length opera in English he ever composed. The libretto was written in collaboration with the great English poet W.H. Auden and Chester Kallman, inspired by William Hogarth's eighteenth-century engraving series of the same name — a moral fable of a young man seduced into degradation and finally madness by the devil-figure Nick Shadow.

Stravinsky built the opera on a framework of Mozartean spare orchestration and Italian Baroque aria structure, constructing a work full of wit and irony. As with his treatment of Pergolesi, his handling of Mozartean idiom is a form of "creative misreading" — harmonies frequently deviate from tonal expectations in subtle ways, rhythms occasionally display non-classical angularity, while the music maintains throughout a cool and refined elegance.

The world premiere on September 11, 1951 at the Teatro La Fenice in Venice was conducted by the composer himself — and it was there, just three weeks later, that Stravinsky met the conductor Robert Craft, who would become his most important collaborator in his late years. The Rake's Progress is the exemplary Neoclassical opera and one of the most important English-language operas of the twentieth century, establishing an unshakeable position in operatic history through its refined musical ingenuity and profound moral allegory.
famousScores.canonicalPerformer: Igor Stravinsky, London Symphony Orchestra, John Eliot Gardiner
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: The Rake's Progress / Act I / Prelude: Prelude

famousScores.name: 《阿贡》（芭蕾）
famousScores.desc: 斯特拉文斯基晚年的序列主义转向，与巴兰钦合作的最纯粹的舞台音乐
famousScores.descEn: Stravinsky's late serial turn — the purest distillation of his collaboration with Balanchine
famousScores.detail:
《阿贡》（希腊语意为「竞争」，1953—1957年）是斯特拉文斯基晚年与编舞家乔治·巴兰钦（George Balanchine）合作最重要的芭蕾作品，也是他向勋伯格的十二音技法靠拢的过渡性杰作。作品共十二段，既有古典舞曲形式（萨拉班德、加沃特），又有纯粹抽象的序列音乐段落，两种语言在同一部作品中奇迹般地和谐共存。

斯特拉文斯基在70岁以后开始认真钻研十二音技法（部分得益于克拉夫特的引导，克拉夫特是一位热情的韦伯恩崇拜者），《阿贡》是他整合新旧两种语言的最成功的尝试。一些段落明显是严格的十二音写作，另一些则保留了新古典主义的音调感，而两者之间的界限，在斯特拉文斯基的手中被处理得极为自然。

1957年6月17日在洛杉矶由罗伯特·克拉夫特指挥世界首演（音乐会版），同年12月由纽约城市芭蕾舞团上演了舞台版，由巴兰钦编舞——那次舞台首演被认为是20世纪芭蕾史上最重要的首演之一。《阿贡》是斯特拉文斯基音乐人生最后阶段的集中体现，也是他证明「任何真正的艺术家永远不会停止学习和进化」的最有力证据。
famousScores.detailEn:
Agon (Greek for "contest"; composed 1953–57) is the most important of Stravinsky's late collaborations with the choreographer George Balanchine, and a transitional masterpiece marking his movement toward Schoenbergian twelve-tone technique. Comprising twelve sections that include both classical dance forms (Sarabande, Gaillarde, Bransle) and purely abstract serial passages, the two languages coexist with miraculous harmony within a single work.

After the age of seventy, Stravinsky began seriously investigating the twelve-tone method — partly through the guidance of Robert Craft, an ardent Webern admirer. Agon is his most successful attempt to integrate his old and new languages. Some passages are clearly strict twelve-tone writing; others retain the tonal feeling of Neoclassicism; and the boundary between the two is handled by Stravinsky with extraordinary naturalness.

The world premiere (concert version) took place in Los Angeles on June 17, 1957, conducted by Robert Craft; the staged production premiered in December 1957 by the New York City Ballet with choreography by Balanchine — one of the most important ballet premieres of the twentieth century. Agon is the concentrated embodiment of the final phase of Stravinsky's musical life and the most powerful evidence that "any truly great artist never stops learning and evolving."
famousScores.canonicalTrackTitle: Agon - Ballet for Twelve Dancers: Prelude

influence: 斯特拉文斯基的影响覆盖了20世纪音乐的几乎每一个角落。他的节奏革命影响了梅西安、布列兹和伯恩斯坦；他的新古典主义启发了普朗克、科普兰和普罗科菲耶夫；他的晚期序列作品证明了十二音技法的开放性。他的管弦乐配器被好莱坞作曲家奉为圭臬。他被T.S.艾略特和奥登等文学巨匠视为同时代最重要的艺术家。他与毕加索并称为20世纪最伟大的两位艺术创新者。
influenceEn: Stravinsky's influence reaches into virtually every corner of twentieth-century music. His rhythmic revolution shaped Messiaen, Boulez, and Bernstein; his Neoclassicism inspired Poulenc, Copland, and Prokofiev; his late serial works demonstrated the openness of the twelve-tone method. Hollywood composers have treated his orchestration as gospel. Literary giants such as T. S. Eliot and Auden considered him the most important artist of their time. He and Picasso are often cited together as the two greatest artistic innovators of the twentieth century.
